perturbation.original_text: The term “ Beneficial Ownership ” when used with reference to a security shall have the meaning ascribed to it under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “1934 Act”), except that for purposes of this definition, the term security shall include any option, warrant, or convertible security regardless of exercise or conversion date, and also include any stock appreciation right, or similar right with an exercise or conversion privilege or a settlement payment or mechanism at a price related to the security or with a value derived in whole or in part from the value of the security, whether or not such instrument or right shall be subject to settlement in securities or otherwise and any other direct or indirect opportunity to profit or share in any profit derived from any increase or decrease in the value of the security
perturbation.changed_text: The term “ Beneficial Ownership ” when used with reference to a security shall have the meaning ascribed to it under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “1934 Act”), except that for purposes of this definition, the term security shall include only common stock directly held by the Recipient and does not include any option, warrant, or convertible security regardless of exercise or conversion date, and also exclude any stock appreciation right, or similar right with an exercise or conversion privilege or a settlement payment or mechanism at a price related to the security or with a value derived in whole or in part from the value of the security, whether or not such instrument or right shall be subject to settlement in securities or otherwise and any other direct or indirect opportunity to profit or share in any profit derived from any increase or decrease in the value of the security
perturbation.explanation: This change narrows the definition of 'Beneficial Ownership' to include only common stock directly held by the Recipient and excludes derivative securities, which contradicts the original broader definition referencing the 1934 Act. This could affect calculations under the Standstill Period (Section 9) since this narrower definition would affect what counts towards beneficial ownership when determining compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees that, for a period of one year after the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), unless specifically invited in writing by Provider, neither it nor any of its affiliates who have been provided Evaluation Material, will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in, or in any way assist any other Person to effect, seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in: (i) any acquisition of any equity securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or all or substantially all of the assets of Provider or any of its subsidiaries, (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving Provider or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to Provider or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of Provider; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined under the 1934 Act) with respect to the securities of Provider; (c) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit an unsolicited proposal for or offer of (with or without condition), any extraordinary transaction involving Provider or its equity securities or assets; (d) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of Provider; (e) take any action which might force Provider to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in (a) above; or (f) enter into any discussions or arrangements with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing. Recipient also agrees during the Standstill Period not to publicly request (or request in a manner or under circumstances that would reasonably require public disclosure of such request) Provider (or its directors, officers, employees or agents), directly or indirectly, to amend or waive any provision of this Section 9 (including this sentence). Recipient further agrees that unless otherwise directed by Provider in writing (i) all communications with the Provider regarding a Possible Transaction, (ii) requests for additional information, facility tours, or management meetings, and (iii) discussions or questions regarding procedures with respect to a Possible Transaction, will be submitted or directed by Recipient or its Representatives only to Ethan Zweig of Citigroup Global Markets Inc., as Provider’s financial advisor, or a person or persons designated in writing by Mr. Zweig, unless otherwise agreed to by the Provider.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Beneficial Ownership' to include only common stock directly held by the Recipient, excluding derivative securities. The original definition, as the original text states, makes a broader reference to the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. This directly contradicts Section 9 (the Standstill Period) because the section refers to beneficial ownership of equity securities. The narrowed definition will impact how the standstill provision is calculated because it excludes derivative securities, which the original clause would have included, thus creating a direct contradiction, based on the explanation given.

perturbation.original_text: The term “ Evaluation Material ” means information (whether oral, written, electronic or otherwise) concerning Provider which has been or is furnished to Recipient or its Representatives (as defined below) by or on behalf of Provider in connection with Recipient’s evaluation of a Possible Transaction, including Provider’s business, financial condition, operations, assets and liabilities, and includes all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by Recipient or its Representatives which contain or are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished by Provider hereunder. The term Evaluation Material does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by Recipient or any of its Representatives in breach of this Agreement, (ii) was within a Recipient’s possession prior to its being furnished to Recipient by or on behalf of the Provider, provided that such information is not known by Recipient or its Representatives (after reasonable inquiry) to be subject to a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, (iii) is or becomes available to Recipient or its Representatives from a source other than Provider or its Representatives, provided that such source is not known by Recipient or its Representatives (after reasonable inquiry) to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, Provider with respect to such information, or (iv) is independently developed by Recipient or its Representatives without use or benefit of the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “ Evaluation Material ” means only written information explicitly labeled as 'Evaluation Material' concerning Provider which has been furnished directly to Recipient by Provider in connection with Recipient’s evaluation of a Possible Transaction. This definition excludes any oral information, electronic data, or any materials derived from the originally furnished documents, including Provider’s business, financial condition, operations, assets and liabilities, and excludes all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by Recipient or its Representatives which contain or are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished by Provider hereunder. The term Evaluation Material does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by Recipient or any of its Representatives in breach of this Agreement, (ii) was within a Recipient’s possession prior to its being furnished to Recipient by or on behalf of the Provider, provided that such information is not known by Recipient or its Representatives (after reasonable inquiry) to be subject to a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, (iii) is or becomes available to Recipient or its Representatives from a source other than Provider or its Representatives, provided that such source is not known by Recipient or its Representatives (after reasonable inquiry) to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, Provider with respect to such information, or (iv) is independently developed by Recipient or its Representatives without use or benefit of the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: This change severely restricts the definition of 'Evaluation Material', limiting it to only written, explicitly labeled information directly furnished by Provider, excluding oral and electronic data and derivative works. This contradicts Section 3, which mandates the confidentiality of all information used for the 'Possible Transaction', including analyses and interpretations. Now it is unclear what is considered confidential between these two sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall, and shall direct its Representatives to, use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating and/or implementing a Possible Transaction, keep the Evaluation Material confidential, and, subject to Section 5, will not, and will direct its Representative not to, disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that any of such information may be disclosed to Recipient’s Representatives who have a good faith need to know such information for the sole purpose of helping Recipient evaluate and/or implement a Possible Transaction; provided, further , that Recipient advises each of its Representatives of the confidentiality terms of this agreement applicable to Representatives. Recipient agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of Recipient’s Representatives. This Agreement does not grant Recipient or any of its Representatives any license to use the Provider’s Evaluation Material except as provided herein. In addition, all proprietary and intellectual property rights in and to the Evaluation Material shall remain the sole property of Provider, and nothing in this Agreement shall be construed in any way to grant to Recipient or its Representatives or any other Person any express or implied option, license or other right, title or interest in or to any Evaluation Material, or to any intellectual property rights embodied in such Evaluation Material. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Vista Equity Partners III, LLC and/or its affiliates is engaged in the purchase and acquisition of, and investment in, software and technology-enabled companies. Accordingly, the mere purchase or acquisition of, or investment in, any other company without otherwise proving that this Agreement has been breached will not be deemed to be a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Evaluation Material' to only explicitly labeled written information furnished directly by the provider. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the recipient to keep 'the Evaluation Material' confidential, implying a broader scope of information covered. The changed text creates a clear in-text contradiction because the contradicted section's confidentiality obligations now apply to a much narrower set of information, creating ambiguity about what is actually confidential.

perturbation.original_text: The term “ Representatives ” shall mean the directors, officers, employees, agents, counsel, accountants, consultants, potential financing sources and advisors of a Party who have a good faith need to know Evaluation Material for purposes of evaluating and/or implementing a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “ Representatives ” shall mean only the specifically named directors and officers of a Party, excluding employees, agents, counsel, accountants, consultants, potential financing sources and advisors, who have a good faith need to know Evaluation Material for purposes of evaluating and/or implementing a Possible Transaction. A list of these approved representatives must be provided to the Provider within 5 business days of the agreement's effective date.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Representatives' is restricted to only specifically named directors and officers, excluding all other categories initially included. This creates a contradiction in Sections 3 and 5 because Recipient's responsibilities related to non-disclosure and legal disclosures are broadened, increasing the scope for disagreement on who represents Recipient during a transaction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall, and shall direct its Representatives to, use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating and/or implementing a Possible Transaction, keep the Evaluation Material confidential, and, subject to Section 5, will not, and will direct its Representative not to, disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that any of such information may be disclosed to Recipient’s Representatives who have a good faith need to know such information for the sole purpose of helping Recipient evaluate and/or implement a Possible Transaction; provided, further , that Recipient advises each of its Representatives of the confidentiality terms of this agreement applicable to Representatives. Recipient agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of Recipient’s Representatives. This Agreement does not grant Recipient or any of its Representatives any license to use the Provider’s Evaluation Material except as provided herein. In addition, all proprietary and intellectual property rights in and to the Evaluation Material shall remain the sole property of Provider, and nothing in this Agreement shall be construed in any way to grant to Recipient or its Representatives or any other Person any express or implied option, license or other right, title or interest in or to any Evaluation Material, or to any intellectual property rights embodied in such Evaluation Material. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Vista Equity Partners III, LLC and/or its affiliates is engaged in the purchase and acquisition of, and investment in, software and technology-enabled companies. Accordingly, the mere purchase or acquisition of, or investment in, any other company without otherwise proving that this Agreement has been breached will not be deemed to be a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Representatives' to only specifically named directors and officers, excluding other categories like employees and agents that were initially included. The contradicted text imposes obligations on the Recipient to direct its 'Representatives' to keep the Evaluation Material confidential and be responsible for any breach by them. By restricting the definition of 'Representatives,' the changed text creates a contradiction because the Recipient's responsibility is less clear in the event of a breach by someone who was previously included in the definition of 'Representatives' but is now excluded, causing uncertainty about who the Recipient is responsible for under the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient understands and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by Recipient or any of its Representatives and that Provider shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction 5 and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threat thereof. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for Provider for a breach by Recipient or its Representatives of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to Provider.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient understands and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of Sections 3, 4 and 9 of this Agreement by Recipient or any of its Representatives and that Provider shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threat thereof. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for Provider for a breach by Recipient or its Representatives of this Agreement, and replaces all other remedies available at law or equity to Provider.
perturbation.explanation: This change restricts equitable relief to breaches of specific sections (3, 4, and 9), but states that these are the exclusive remedies for _any_ breach of the agreement. The term 'exclusive remedies' means this is the only remedy for those sections, the contradiction arises when stating, this is the only remedy for those breaches and _replaces_ all other available at law or equity to Provider for _breaches to the entire agreement_. This contradicts the principle that all other remedies are available at law or equity to Provider, because of _breaches to the entire agreement_, creating ambiguity on how violations outside those sections are to be handled.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient understands and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of Sections 3, 4 and 9 of this Agreement by Recipient or any of its Representatives and that Provider shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threat thereof. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for Provider for a breach by Recipient or its Representatives of this Agreement, and replaces all other remedies available at law or equity to Provider.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that equitable relief is the 'exclusive remed[y] for a breach by Recipient or its Representatives of this Agreement, and replaces all other remedies available at law or equity to Provider' while the original text stated that equitable relief 'shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for Provider for a breach by Recipient or its Representatives of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to Provider.' This directly contradicts the remedies available, creating an in-text contradiction, adhering to the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction by defining obligations inconsistently.

perturbation.original_text: Provider and Recipient shall each be referred to herein individually, as a “ Party ” and collectively, as the “ Parties .”
perturbation.changed_text: Provider shall be referred to herein individually, as a “ Party, ” Vista Equity Partners III, LLC and its affiliates shall be reffered to as Recipient and not included in Party or Parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes Recipient and its affiliates from being recognized as a 'Party'. In this case the in-text contradiction takes place in the whole document since the agreement is between both, this agreement can't take place if the agreement only applies to the Provider and all of the obligations would only apply to the Provider and not the Recipient.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the Parties regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings, arrangements and discussions between the Parties regarding such subject matter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes Recipient (Vista Equity Partners III, LLC and its affiliates) from the definition of 'Party' and 'Parties'. The contradicted text states that 'This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the Parties'. If the Recipient is not considered a Party, then the entire agreement between both parties is undermined, which would create a contradiction in whether the agreement has validity or not. This fits the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition because it makes the term 'Parties' inconsistent and unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient acknowledges that the Evaluation Material may include material nonpublic information (within the meaning of the securities laws of the United States) with respect to Provider.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient acknowledges that the Evaluation Material may include material nonpublic information (within the meaning of the securities laws of the United States) with respect to Provider, but the Provider ensures that it will disclose material public information to Recipient with respect to Provider.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the statement, “but the Provider ensures that it will disclose material public information to Recipient with respect to Provider,” it creates an in-text contradiction because material nonpublic information are facts not to be disclosed, but material public information are facts that must be disclosed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient acknowledges that the Evaluation Material may include material nonpublic information (within the meaning of the securities laws of the United States) with respect to Provider, but the Provider ensures that it will disclose material public information to Recipient with respect to Provider.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a contradiction because it states that the Evaluation Material may include material nonpublic information, but then adds that the Provider ensures it will disclose material public information. Nonpublic information is, by definition, not to be disclosed, while public information is meant to be disclosed. This directly contradicts the nature of nonpublic information, creating an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, reinforcing the contradiction within the same section, which is still considered an in-text contradiction according to the instructions.
